mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate keeps real discount rates low and supports equity valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when policy stays at or near the lower bound.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns when FEDFUNDS is unchanged or falling, or if the triggered long-only signal has negative average return over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions, raises the discount rate, and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or if the average 20-day return after the trigger is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE with weak PAYEMS growth signals labor-market deterioration, which lowers expected household income and risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days are negative when unemployment is climbing and payroll gains are slowing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months and PAYEMS growth is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-PAYEMS trigger does not produce negative average 60-day forward returns, or if the sign is not worse than using UNRATE alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating RSAFS indicates resilient consumer demand and revenue growth for large-cap firms, which supports higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth is positive for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive positive RSAFS growth does not precede above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal's rank correlation with forward returns is indistinguishable from zero over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, firms are expanding real output without a parallel inflation shock, which should lower margin pressure and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and stable CPIAUCSL do not outperform baseline on 60-day SPX returns, or if inflation acceleration eliminates the positive sign in subsample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after weakness signals housing-cycle stabilization and future demand spillovers into consumption and industrial activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases month over month after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebound episodes fail to deliver positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if returns remain below unconditional levels after the trigger over any rolling 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and PAYEMS confirms broad demand reacceleration and earnings stabilization, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days are positive only when both series improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-PAYEMS improvement does not outperform either series alone on 60-day forward returns, or if the combined signal loses positive average return in recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: During inflation shocks, a faster CPIAUCSL print combined with a still-near-zero FEDFUNDS rate implies policy is behind the curve, raising real-rate uncertainty and compressing equity multiples, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes with low FEDFUNDS do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus CPIAUCSL alone.
